title: Tapping to Live in Daily Overflow
subtitle: A tapping practice for anchoring overflow as an ordinary, daily expectation
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 63 of a 12-week money program. The script targets the belief that overflow is occasional — a good month, a lucky break, a temporary state. The reframe moves toward overflow as the natural daily expectation rather than the exception.
sourceNotes: The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from Day 63 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you feel that financial ease belongs to certain seasons and that the current season is not one of them, or when overflow feels like something you are waiting for rather than something you are already in.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 63 of a 12-week money program. The script targets the temporary-overflow belief: that financial ease is a visiting state rather than a permanent one. The reframe moves toward overflow as the daily baseline — what you wake up expecting, not what you are waiting for.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though overflow once felt rare and temporary, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I worried it would disappear, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I couldn't picture overflow as my daily life, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: I used to think overflow was for special occasions.
Side of eye: I felt surprised when it happened.
Under eye: I didn't let it feel normal.
Under nose: I feared it would end.
Chin: I now choose a different expectation.
Collarbone: I make overflow my everyday baseline.
Under arm: I enjoy seeing surplus in every area.
Top of head: I live with constant financial plenty.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: Overflow is my natural daily state.
Side of eye: I wake up knowing there is more than enough.
Under eye: I see abundance in every account and corner.
Under nose: I trust money to always exceed what I need.
Chin: I feel calm, generous, and free.
Collarbone: I let wealth keep rising without limit.
Under arm: I celebrate life as overflowing every day.
Top of head: I live in permanent, joyful financial plenty.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from

The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy. The script is adapted from Day 63 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
